summary: This brief post recommends Focus@Will, a music streaming service designed to enhance concentration while working or studying. The author shares that the service uses neuroscience-based music channels that aim to engage the brain's limbic system without causing distraction. The post includes marketing claims about the science behind the approach, citing partnerships with neuroscientists. Simon personally endorses it based on his experience using it during his bachelor's degree work.
key_points: Focus@Will offers music channels designed by neuroscientists to improve concentration
Mainstream music is argued to be counterproductive for focus because it's designed to engage emotionally and intellectually
The service claims to increase focus biomarkers by 12-15% and extend work session times significantly
The author found personal success using it during academic project work
money_quotes: Mainstream music lowers comprehension and creates distraction because it is designed to connect with you intellectually and emotionally.
It was unbelievable how focused and concentrated I worked on my project.
key_insight: Purpose-designed background music may be more effective for concentration than mainstream music because it avoids the emotional and intellectual engagement that causes distraction.
